title: phyllanthus aubletianus
intro: Phyllanthus aubletianus, also known as the West Indian gooseberry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. The plant is traditionally used in medicine, particularly in the Caribbean, due to its antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are derived from its leaves and fruits. Phyllanthus aubletianus has been reported to have several health benefits, including the reduction of liver damage, improvement of immune function, and management of diabetes. The plant is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional remedies and pharmaceutical formulations.
